data.title: Morning News Wrap-Up: Friday's Biggest Stock Market Stories!
data.description:
The end of the trading week is here, and investors only need to catch up on Friday's news before signing off for the weekend. TipRanks is here to help speed that up with a breakdown of the biggest market happenings today.

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.

  • Wheeler Real Estate Investment (WHLR) stock soared on a strong earnings report.
  • SoFi Technologies (SOFI) stock fell on plans for a share sale.
  • Parsons (PSN) stock plummeted after the company failed to secure a government contract.
  • Strategy (MSTR) stock was hit by a price target cut from a top analyst.
  • Nvidia (NVDA) stock received continued support from a highly rated analyst.
  • The S&P 500 (SPX) closed in on a new high alongside the latest PCE report.
  • Jeffs' Brands (JFBR) stock gained this morning on an exclusive deal with Scanary.
  • The New York Times (NYT) stock remained flat despite news of a lawsuit against Perplexity AI (PERPL).
  • The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) gained alongside the most recent PCE report.
  • Lumen Technologies (LUMN) stock was on the move after the company announced a leadership change.
  • Delta Air Lines (DAL) stock slipped after it announced a disruption in Detroit.
  • Apple (AAPL) stock was down after the tech giant issued a global cybersecurity warning.
  • Rubrik (RBRK) stock surged following the cloud data management company's latest earnings report.
  • Bank of America (BAC) offered a bullish take on the stock market as it prepares for an interest rate cut.
  • Netflix (NFLX) has reached a deal to acquire Warner Bros. from Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD).
  • Netflix's deal also includes Warner Bros. game division and will bolster its gaming studio.
  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock gained on details of how much GPU prices will increase.

Read more of the top stock market stories at TipRanks.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Advance on Soft Inflation Data ahead of S&P 500 Rebalancing Update
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed in green territory ahead of the weekend.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The market got a boost after September's core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index rose by 2.8% year-over-year, below the consensus estimate of 2.9%. Core PCE is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation and excludes volatile food and energy prices. Headline PCE increased by 2.8% from a year ago, matching the estimate. In addition, both year-ahead and long-run inflation expectations fell to 11-month lows in a survey conducted by the University of Michigan. Consumers now expect year-ahead inflation of 4.1%, down from 4.5%, and long-run inflation of 3.2%, down from 3.4%.

Meanwhile, consumer sentiment rose in early December, ending a streak of five consecutive months of declines. December's preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment tallied at 53.3, rising from 51.0 in November and ahead of the consensus estimate of 52.0. At the same time, the index is still down by 28% compared to a year ago. A reading of 80 or higher signals optimism, while a reading of 60 or below signals pessimism.

"Overall, while views of current conditions were little changed, expectations improved, led by a 13% rise in expected personal finances, with improvements visible across age, income, education, and political affiliation," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu.

S&P Dow Jones Indices is set to announce new additions to the S&P 500 (SPX), if any, after the market close. The benchmark index undergoes rebalancing each quarter, with the new fourth-quarter changes effective as of December 19. Companies must meet a range of requirements in order to be considered for inclusion, such as a market capitalization of at least $22.7 billion and a minimum monthly trading volume of at least 250,000 shares during the past six months.

Stephens analyst Melissa Roberts believes that Comfort Systems (FIX), Pure Storage (PSTG), and Ciena (CIEN), the three largest companies in the S&P Midcap 400 index, are strong contenders. KBW analyst Shreyank Gandhi pointed to Vertiv Holdings (VRT), CRH (CRH), Alnylam Pharmaceuticals (ALNY), and Ares Management (ARES) as potential candidates given their large market caps. Gandhi also floated the possibility of Carvana (CVNA), SoFi Technologies (SOFI), and Strategy (MSTR) being added to the benchmark index.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.19% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.43%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'This Isn't What You Think,' Says Top BofA Analyst About Nvidia Stock
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) has recently faced several questions about demand, supply limits, and even warnings of an "AI bubble." But Bank of America's (Bofa) top analyst Vivek Arya said the worry is off the mark. In a new note, he pushed back on the slowdown fears and said the market has the story "completely wrong," pointing to new signs of strong demand coming out of Amazon's (AMZN) recent AWS event.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The 5-star analyst maintained his Overweight rating on the stock and a price target of $275, which suggests about 50% upside from current levels. It is worth noting that Arya ranks 203 out of more than 10,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 59%, with an average return per rating of 17.4% over a one-year timeframe.

AWS Still Leans on Nvidia, Says Analyst

Arya said the latest AWS updates show that Nvidia remains at the center of cloud AI systems. AWS plans to use NVLink Fusion in its next wave of chips, including its Trainium 4 accelerator expected in 2026–27. He said this shows that even big cloud firms building their own chips still rely on Nvidia's core tools.

He added that NVLink Fusion will help AWS scale its new servers through Nvidia's MGX designs, giving Nvidia another long-term win inside one of the world's largest cloud platforms.

Arya said that AWS comments eased "AI bubble" fears, showing that Amazon's cloud unit is still expanding its AI spend and that Nvidia is a key part of that push.

Nvidia Repeats Strong Demand Outlook

The analyst pointed out that Nvidia also spoke at a broker event and repeated that it sees about $500 billion in data center sales over 2025 and 2026.

He stressed that this number does not include the large OpenAI deal, which has not been signed yet. Arya said this adds even more support to Nvidia's long-term story and shows that demand could end up higher than what the market expects.

Is Nvidia a Buy or Sell Stock?  

According to TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $258.10, the Nvidia average share price target implies almost 43% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Lumen Technologies Stock (LUMN) Shifts on an Executive Change
data.description:
Lumen Technologies (LUMN) stock was on the move Friday after the company revealed a change to its executive team. Jim Fowler is taking over as the Chief Technology & Product Officer at Lumen Technologies starting on January 5, 2026. This will have him replace Dave Ward, who is leaving the role to become the President and Chief Architect at Salesforce (CRM).

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

When Fowler takes on his new role at Lumen Technologies, he will report directly to CEO Kate Johnson. His duties will include management of the company's global technology and product strategy. Fowler will be aided by Ward at first, who will stick around until January 23, 2026, to ensure a smooth transition. Investors will also note that Fowler isn't new to Lumen Technologies, having served on its board of directors since 2023. He left his board position in relation to his new role at the company.

Lumen Technologies made sure to note that its technology roadmap won't change with the departure of Ward. Johnson reiterated this when she stated, "Dave's visionary leadership has laid a foundation that will influence Lumen's trajectory for years ahead, and we thank him for his service." Ward's work experience includes two decades at General Electric, which has since split into GE Aerospace (GE) and GE Vernova (GEV).

Lumen Technologies Stock Movement Today

Lumen Technologies stock was down earlier on Friday morning, but has recovered since then and is now up slightly. The stock has also rallied 65.35% year-to-date and 25.43% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: AMZN, ETSY, GOOGL, META, & RDDT: Truist Analyst Sees These Stocks Benefiting from Record Holiday E-Commerce Spending
data.description:
Truist analyst Youssef Squali said 2025's U.S. holiday shopping season looks strong for online retailers and digital advertisers. In an investor note, Squali said spending trends point to record levels of e-commerce sales and digital ad outlays, with Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOGL), Meta (META), Reddit (RDDT), and Etsy (ETSY) among the biggest beneficiaries.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Strong Start to the Season

Squali highlighted that aggregate holiday spending through December 1, including Cyber Week, is running ahead of expectations. Adobe (ADBE) reported Cyber Week sales of $44.25 billion, up 7.7% year‑over‑year and above its forecast of $43.7 billion.

Overall holiday spending from November 1 to December 1 is up 7.1% year‑over‑year, outpacing Adobe's 5.3% estimate.

Discounting has been a major theme, with retailers launching promotions earlier and across more categories. Squali noted that more efficient digital ad channels powered by AI are also driving incremental traffic to e-commerce sites. Also, he believes that strong consumer demand and low unemployment have led to record spending levels.

How Companies Are Expected to Benefit

  • Amazon: The analyst estimates Amazon's share of U.S. e-commerce will climb to 50% in 2025, up from 49% in 2024.
  • Etsy: He said that early data shows Etsy performing as expected or better.
  • Digital Ad Winners: Squali noted that Google, Meta, and Reddit are expected to benefit from increased ad spending as retailers invest more in search, social, connected TV, and retail media.

Which Stock Is Best to Buy Now?

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Analysts Reaffirm Buy Rating on Amazon Stock (AMZN), Citing AWS Growth Potential
data.description:
Following Amazon's (AMZN) annual AWS (Amazon Web Services) re:Invent conference held from December 1 to 5, several analysts reiterated their bullish stance on AMZN stock. In particular, they noted artificial intelligence (AI)-led growth potential in AWS. The e-commerce and cloud computing giant made several key announcements at the event, including the rollout of new Nova GenAI models. Let's look at the views of some analysts on AWS' updates.  

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Analysts Are Confident About AWS' Growth

Following the updates at the event, TD Cowen analyst John Blackledge reiterated a Buy rating on Amazon stock with a price target of $300. The 5-star analyst noted the key launches at the event, including new Nova GenAI models (such as Omni) and custom Trainium 3 UltraServers, with up to 50% cost reduction and up to 4.4x higher compute performance than Trainium 2. He also mentioned new agentic AI features like AgentCore and QuickSuite, as well as the update on Zoox rideshare AVs (autonomous vehicles), which run on AWS.

Blackledge expects AWS to gain from continued revenue acceleration, driven by the broader AI boom. He expects AWS revenue to grow 19.1% to $128.1 billion in 2025, increase by 22.9% in 2026, and by 23.9% in 2027. Overall, the analyst expects AWS revenue to grow at a CAGR (compound annual growth rate) of 22% in the 2025-2030 period to $348.5 billion. Furthermore, he expects AWS' operating income to increase to $45.9 billion in 2025 and $117.8 billion in 2030, reflecting a 21% CAGR over the five-year tenure.

Likewise, Goldman Sachs analyst Eric Sheridan reiterated a Buy rating on AMZN stock with a price target of $290. Sheridan stated that Amazon's recent earnings report and the AWS re:Invent event reinforced the company's ability to re-accelerate top-line growth and the role that the cloud unit will play as the AI theme evolves. The 4-star analyst continues to believe that AWS can generate revenue growth at a CAGR of around "20%+" over the next three years.

Also, KeyBanc analyst Justin Patterson reiterated a Buy rating on Amazon stock with a price target of $303. Commenting on the AWS re:Invent event, the analyst said that he is "encouraged" by Amazon's initiatives to provide more flexible AI offerings, from models to silicon deployments. "Customer choice has long been a core tenet of AWS, and our customer conversations suggest reception to AWS is improving," said Patterson. Given the company's efforts to boost AWS capacity over the next two years, Patterson expects revenue growth of "+21%" into 2026.

What Is the Target Price for Amazon Stock?

With 43 Buys versus just one Hold rating, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Amazon stock. The average AMZN stock price target of $295.63 indicates about 29% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: RBC Capital Stays Cautious on Rivian Stock (RIVN) as Autonomy and AI Day Approaches
data.description:
Rivian (RIVN) stock has risen 35% year-to-date, driven by optimism about the electric vehicle (EV) maker's upcoming R2 SUV launch and strong third-quarter results. Ahead of the company's Autonomy and AI Day event on December 11, RBC Capital analyst Tom Narayan reiterated a Hold rating on RIVN stock with a price target of $14, saying that it is still a "show-me story."

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Here's Why RBC Capital Analyst Is Cautious on Rivian Stock

Narayan praised Rivian's shift toward autonomy, as he believes that Level 3 will be a crucial move for all automakers. "Its goal of in-sourcing could make autonomy a profit center, which is important, especially given the company's liquidity situation," added the analyst.

Furthermore, Narayan expects Rivian to adopt a subscription model like larger rival Tesla (TSLA), but with lower pricing. The 4-star analyst noted that this approach contrasts with that of Mercedes (MBGAF) and BMW (BAMXF), which seem to be using autonomy to bolster their product offerings rather than viewing it as a significant profit driver.

Narayan also likes the segments that Rivian is focused on. He finds Rivian's initial consumer products, the R1T and R1S, "very impressive and category-defining." However, Narayan noted that Rivian is still generating losses and could face intense competition from legacy original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Moreover, the ongoing slowdown in the EV market adds to the company's woes. While the Volkswagen (VWAGY) deal helped Rivian address liquidity issues, Narayan believes that the success of the R2/R3 models will be crucial for the company.

"All that said, while we see December 11's event as important, being able to scale R2/R3 profitably will be far more critical for investors," concluded Narayan.

Is Rivian Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Currently, Wall Street is sidelined on Rivian Automotive stock based on five Buys, eight Holds, and five Sells. The average RIVN stock price target of $13.98 indicates 22.1% downside risk.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: 'It's Not Forever,' Says Investor About Nvidia Stock
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) didn't become the leader in AI chips by accident – it earned that position by building hardware and software that define the standard for the industry. Today, it holds a commanding presence, with its share of the AI accelerator market estimated at more than 90%.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Looking ahead, one investor, who goes by the pseudonym Hunting Alpha, believes its leading revenue indicators are "very robust."

Revenue trends at Foxconn, which supplies a major chunk of Nvidia's AI hardware, are on the up. Moreover, Nvidia is signaling that its long-term revenue growth prospects are strong, as highlighted by a year-over-year acceleration in remaining performance obligations (RPO), particularly for net new RPOs beyond the next twelve months, which increased by $360 million sequentially. The company also anticipates generating up to $500 billion in revenue from Blackwell and next-generation Rubin chips by the end of calendar year 2026.

However, looking at the bigger picture, Hunting Alpha thinks that competitive pressures appear to be building, and Nvidia's leverage with customers might be slipping. One sign is the growing concentration of receivables tied to its largest cloud-service-provider customers. That share has climbed from an average of 23.8% in FY2020–2024 to 65% in Q3 FY26, suggesting Nvidia may be in a somewhat weaker bargaining position than before.

Several major customers are also working to lessen their dependence on Nvidia. Microsoft is pushing its in-house Maia accelerators to replace Nvidia's chips, while Amazon is leaning into its Trainium and Inferentia designs, which can offer 30–40% lower costs than Nvidia's GPUs. OpenAI is exploring a custom-chip partnership with Broadcom, and Google's TPUs now present perhaps the most serious challenge, delivering a lower total cost of ownership than Nvidia's Blackwell chips at certain performance levels.

Additionally, from a technical standpoint, when comparing Nvidia's stock performance to the S&P 500 on relative monthly charts, Hunting Alpha is seeing signs of "bullish momentum slowing down."

So, viewed in isolation, the investor says Nvidia's results and guide suggest its revenue momentum remains very strong. But looking at how its major customers are behaving tells a different story. For investors who are bullish on the stock, this should be a "big sign of caution," even though Nvidia is still a generation ahead of rivals. With the current pace of innovation, however, a "generation" now lasts only about a year.

From a valuation standpoint, Hunting Alpha still finds NVDA appealing, as the shares trade at an unusually attractive discount relative to peers. "However," the investor summed up, "this plus point is arguably offset by slowing momentum in NVDA stock."

And with the view that Nvidia's advantage may not last forever, the investor issues a Hold (i.e., Neutral) rating rather than a Buy. (To watch Hunting Alpha's track record, click here)

That view stands in contrast to the Street sentiment. Only one analyst shares that Neutral stance, and just one rates NVDA a Sell. The remaining 39 recent opinions are firmly bullish, resulting in a Strong Buy consensus rating. With an average price target of $258.10, the analysts see 41% upside over the next year. (See NVDA stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: 'Don't Sleep on Alphabet Stock.' Top Analyst Hikes Price Target to Street-High $400
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) stock just earned a Street-high price target from Pivotal Research. In a new note, top Analyst Michael Levine kept his Buy rating and raised his target to $400 from $350, saying Alphabet is beginning to separate itself from rivals. The new price target suggests about 26% upside from current levels.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Levine said Search remains a reliable profit driver with strong pricing power. He also called Gemini one of the strongest AI systems today, supported by years of work and data that few companies can match. With more searches now routed through Gemini on phones, Alphabet keeps more profit. Apple (AAPL) even pays for access to Gemini, a change that lifts margins and gives Alphabet more room to invest.

Why He Thinks Alphabet Pulls Ahead

Levine believes Gemini will gain even more ground in 2026. With more than five billion users across Alphabet's products, he said Gemini has a built-in distribution lead no rival can match. Most people stick with default apps, which could make Gemini the everyday AI tool for phones and glasses.

He also warned that the picture may get tougher for OpenAI if these trends continue. Rising expenses and slowing growth could force it to cut spending next year, which might lead to a choppy period for AI stocks. But he said Alphabet is well-positioned to handle that.

Levine also pointed to Alphabet's TPUs as a key advantage. He said they offer cheaper AI computing than Nvidia's (NVDA) GPUs and could win more large customers. Over time, he sees TPUs either taking share from Nvidia or pushing GPU prices lower, both of which help Alphabet.

To Levine, the message is clear. Alphabet has the users, the data, and the AI. And the company's lead, he said, is getting harder to ignore. He expects revenue, profits, and free cash flow to keep improving from here.

Is GOOGL Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Alphabet stock based on 29 Buys and seven Hold recommendations. The average GOOGL stock price target of $318.48 indicates shares are fully priced at current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Cathie Wood Cuts Stakes in Tesla and Meta Stocks Again, Loads Up on Baidu and Trade Desk (TTD)
data.description:
Cathie Wood made several notable trades across her ARK ETFs on Friday, December 5, as daily trade filings showed new selling in two key "Magnificent 7" stocks, Tesla (TSLA) and Meta (META). At the same time, ARK bought new shares in Baidu (BIDU) and The Trade Desk (TTD). The filings indicate Wood is redirecting capital from crowded large-cap trades to mid-cap tech stocks she sees as better positioned for 2026.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Wood Continues to Trim Stakes in TSLA and META

The biggest reduction came from Tesla, where ARK sold 37,878 shares through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK). The sale was worth $17.2 million and extended a steady trend of trimming one of Wood's long-time high-conviction holdings.

ARK also cut its stake in Meta, selling 9,200 shares for about $6.1 million. Meta has now been reduced several times over the past few sessions, reflecting a steady shift away from mega-cap tech.

ARK continued to unwind positions in other names as well. The fund sold 223,158 shares of Iridium Communications (IRDM) for $3.9 million and offloaded 195,141 shares of Adaptive Biotechnologies (ADPT) through ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG) for about $3.4 million.

Wood Loads Up on Baidu, TTD, and Other Names

On the buying side, Baidu was one of the biggest additions. ARK bought 119,982 Baidu shares worth $14.24 million through ARKK. The move adds fresh exposure to a major AI and cloud name in China after being careful about that market for most of the year.

Wood also purchased 180,445 shares of Trade Desk, split across ARKK and the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW), for about $7.1 million. ARK has been adding to its Trade Desk position as digital ad spending picks up and more advertisers look for targeted, AI-based tools.

Beyond the headline trades, ARK also increased exposure to several other growth names:

  • GeneDx (WGS): Bought 51,496 shares across ARKK and ARKG worth $8.34 million
  • WeRide (WRD): Added 35,991 shares through ARKQ for about $336,875
  • Robinhood (HOOD): Picked up 9,400 shares valued at $1.29 million

The additional buys show continued interest in AI, autonomous driving, and next-generation fintech.
